new_deals_notified.merger_name: Eagers Vic - Audi Melbourne and Audi Richmond
new_deals_notified.merger_description: Eagers Vic Pty Ltd (**Eagers**) is a subsidiary of Eagers Automotive Limited (ASX: APE), a publicly listed automotive retail group which owns and operates motor vehicle dealerships across Australia. Eagers Automotive Limited sells new and used motor vehicles through its dealerships, representing around 50 automotive brands. Eagers also provides related goods and services including motor vehicle service, parts and allied consumer finance. Eagers operates 12 dealerships in the Melbourne area, including in Toorak (Mercedes-Benz), Malvern (ULR Jaguar and ULR Land Rover) and Port Melbourne (Jaguar, Volvo, Volkswagen and Land Rover). Eagers does not currently have any Audi dealerships in Victoria.

deals_cleared.merger_description: CARe Autobody Pty Ltd (**CARe**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in MotorOne Autobody Pty Ltd (**MotorOne Autobody**) (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: CARe - MotorOne Autobody - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CARe Autobody Pty Ltd (CARe) proposes to acquire 100% of the
issued share capital in MotorOne Autobody Pty Ltd (MotorOne
Autobody) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

CARe is an Australian supplier of motor vehicle collision repair
services. CARe operates a total of 18 repair sites across Australia
with 5 sites in Queensland, 7 sites in Victoria, 3 sites in New South
Wales, 2 sites in Tasmania and 1 site in South Australia.
MotorOne Autobody is an Australian supplier of motor vehicle
collision repair services and a business division of the MotorOne
Group, which is an Australian supplier of motor vehicle aftercare
products and services. MotorOne Autobody operates a total of 20
repair sites in Australia with 8 sites in Queensland, 11 sites in
Victoria and 1 site in New South Wales.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
CARe and MotorOne Autobody overlap in the supply of motor vehicle
collision repair services.
Both CARe and MotorOne Autobody supply motor vehicle collision
repair services to insurers and direct to vehicle owners without an
insurance claim.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, publicly available information, and feedback from
market participants.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• Aggregation in the supply of motor vehicle collision repair
services resulting from the Acquisition appears low at national,
state and local levels.
• The merged entity would likely continue to be constrained by
alternative suppliers of motor vehicle collision repair services in
each area where their businesses overlap in Queensland,
Victoria and New South Wales post-acquisition.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Rocket Software, Inc.(**Rocket Software**), a portfolio company of Bain Capital, proposes to acquire the Vertica Analytics Database Platform (**Vertica**) from Open Text Corporation (**Open Text**). The proposed acquisition will occur largely by way of asset sale.

events.title: Rocket Vertica - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Rocket Software, Inc. (Rocket Software)’s proposed acquisition of
the Vertica Analytics Database Platform (Vertica) from Open Text
Corporation (Open Text) (the Acquisition). The Acquisition will occur
largely by way of asset sale.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Rocket Software, is a portfolio company of Bain
Capital. Rocket Software is a global provider of infrastructure and
application software solutions. Rocket Software’s products and
services focus on modernising and optimising applications on
mainframe computers. Bain Capital is a private equity investment
firm that invests in companies across a number of industries,
including information technology, healthcare, retail and consumer
products, communications, financial services and
industrial/manufacturing.
The target, Vertica, is an enterprise-grade analytical database
software that currently forms part of Open Text. Vertica is designed
for large-scale data analytics within a customer’s own IT
environment.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
While Rocket Software and Vertica (together, the Parties) both
supply business analytics software, there is limited functional
overlap between their products. In particular, unlike Vertica, neither
Rocket Software, nor any other connected entity of Rocket Software
provides a dedicated platform for large-scale data analytics
workloads.
The ACCC understands that post acquisition, Vertica’s capabilities
could be used to improve certain aspects of Rocket Software’s
offerings with data analytics-related functionality. However, the
ACCC has not identified any other actual vertical or conglomerate
relationships between Vertica and Rocket Software or any other Bain
Capital portfolio company (or other connected entity of Rocket
Software) active in the technology sector. However, the Acquisition
could potentially give rise to a vertical relationship in future if one of
Rocket’s connected entities (including a company managed by Bain
Capital) were to become a supplier or customer of the Vertica
product.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• There is no (or very limited) horizontal overlap between the
product offerings of Rocket Software and Vertica (including the
product offerings of the parties’ connected entities), given
differences in functionality.
• Post acquisition, Rocket Software would be unlikely to have an
ability and incentive to foreclose rivals’ access to a large-scale
data analytics platform, including by adopting an anti-
competitive bundling or tying strategy, given the availability of
multiple alternative suppliers of analytical database platforms,
including Microsoft, Oracle and Databricks.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

events.title: Argo Bowen 2 – Bowen Coking Coal and certain subsidiaries - Phase 1 Determination
events.display_title: Argo Bowen 2 – Bowen Coking Coal and certain subsidiaries - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Argo Bowen 2 Pty Ltd (Argo Bowen 2) proposes to acquire 100% of
the shares in Bowen Coking Coal Limited (ASX:BCB) (BCC) and
certain wholly owned subsidiaries (together, the Bowen Group)
pursuant to a Deed of Company Arrangement Proposal dated 9
February 2026 (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Argo Bowen 2, is a subsidiary of Argo Bowen Pty Ltd,
who is currently wholly owned by Argo Holdings QLD Pty Ltd, who is
owned by Mercuria Argo Investments Pty Limited (Mercuria) and
Eclipse Mining 2 Pty Ltd (Eclipse)
Another subsidiary of Argo Holdings QLD Pty Ltd has entered into a
contract to acquire a 70% interest in Fitzroy (CQ) Pty Ltd (the Fitzroy
Group), which owns and operates three mines in the Bowen Basin
region: Carborough Downs, Ironbark No. 1 mine and Broadlea open
cut mine. The Fitzroy Group principally produces metallurgical coal.
Mercuria is a subsidiary of Mercuria Energy Group Limited, an energy
and commodity trading group, specialising in physical commodities,
investing in strategic assets, providing financing and logistics
services.
Eclipse is a subsidiary of Talisman Partners Pty Ltd (Talisman
Partners). Talisman, through another subsidiary, Eclipse Mining Pty
Ltd, provides mine contracting and operations support which
focuses on building and operating mines on behalf of mine owners.
The target, BCC, is a company that focuses on the development,
production and sale of metallurgical and thermal coal. BCC
appointed voluntary administrators on 30 July 2025.
BCC is the indirect majority investor in the Burton-Lenton Joint
Venture, which, relevant to the Acquisition, owns:
▪ the Burton Mining Complex (also known as the New Burton
Mine), which owns the Ellensfield South Mine and Plumtree
North Mine;
▪ the Broadmeadow East Mine (a satellite pit);
▪ a coal handling and processing plant and associated
facilities including a train loadout facility, rail loop and haul
road.
The Bowen Group also owns the Bluff Mine, which is excluded from
the Acquisition.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
Horizontal
The Fitzroy Group and the Bowen Group overlap in the supply of
metallurgical coal.
Non-horizontal
Talisman Partners is a potential supplier of mine contracting and
operations services to other coal mines.
Mercuria is a potential customer of coal mines.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Acquisition will have limited impact on domestic sales of
coal in Australia as most of the coal produced by the Fitzroy
Group and the Bowen Group in Australia is exported to overseas
customers.
• The market share aggregation in either metallurgical or thermal
coal production is estimated to be low on any market definition,
with an estimated post-acquisition market share of below 5%.
• Talisman Partners is unlikely to have the ability and incentive to
foreclose downstream rivals’ access to mining operation
services post-acquisition given alternative providers (including
Xcoal Mining Services, Mining Pro, Thiess and Buma) are able to
compete to provide mining services at mining sites.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Rosewood AG, LP, a Guernsey limited partnership (indirectly owned by the Eighth Cinven Fund) proposes to acquire voting rights in Collagen Holdings, LLC (trading as **Regenity**) (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Rosewood AG, LP (Rosewood) applied for a notification waiver in
relation to its proposed acquisition of joint control over the voting
rights in Collagen Holdings LLC (trading as Regenity), alongside the
existing investor, Linden Capital Partners, as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
1 April 2026

The acquirer, Rosewood, is a Guernsey limited partnership indirectly
owned by the Eighth Cinven Fund, which is indirectly managed and
controlled by Cinven Limited. Cinven Limited has exclusive authority
to make investment and management decisions for Eighth Cinven
Fund. Eighth Cinven Fund and Cinven Limited are part of Cinven.
Cinven is a private equity firm engaged in the provision of investment
management and investment advisory services to a number of
investment funds. The controlled companies in Cinven’s portfolio of
investments are active in a variety of sectors including business
services, consumer, financial services, healthcare, industrials and
technology, media and telecommunications.
The target, Regenity, is a global developer and manufacturer of
bioresorbable technologies. The company is headquartered in
Paramus, New Jersey.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular, there is no competitive overlap or
complementary relationship between Cinven and its connected
entities and Regenity in the supply of bioresorbable technologies in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_name: Permira and Warburg Pincus - Clearwater Analytics
deals_cleared.merger_description: Permira is a Guernsey registered private equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment management services to a number of investment funds. Permira ultimately controls a number of private equity funds, which include portfolio companies active across the consumer, services, healthcare and technology sectors.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
GT Silver BidCo, Inc applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of share capital in Clearwater Analytics
Holdings, Inc (Clearwater Analytics), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
GT Silver BidCo, Inc is a wholly owned subsidiary of GT Silver Parent
LP, which will be jointly controlled by funds managed and advised by
subsidiaries or affiliates of Permira Holdings Limited (Permira) and
Warburg Pincus LLC (along with other affiliates acquiring non-
controlling stakes, including Francisco Partners and Temasek).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
31 March 2026

Permira is a Guernsey registered private equity business engaged,
through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment
management services to a number of investment funds. Permira
ultimately controls a number of private equity funds, which include
portfolio companies active across the consumer, services,
healthcare and technology sectors.
Warburg Pincus LLC is private equity firm headquartered in New
York, USA. Warburg Pincus LLC’s portfolio companies are active in a
variety of sectors, including consumer, energy, financial services,
healthcare, industrial and business services and technology, media
and telecommunications. In Australia, Warburg Pincus LLC’s
portfolio companies are primarily active in software and financial
sectors.
The target, Clearwater Analytics, a software-as-a-service company,
headquartered in Boise, Idaho, USA, that provides automated, cloud-
based software solutions for investors to manage investment data.
Clearwater Analytics provides accounting and reporting, data,
advanced analytics, risk and performance and portfolio and order
management capabilities for a range of investor types across both
public and private markets.
Clearwater Analytics’ platform services the following clients: asset
managers, banks, corporates, energy (e.g., commodity trading),
hedge funds, insurance companies, pensions and endowments, and
the public sector (i.e., government entities managing public funds).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the parties in the
supply of investment, accounting and reporting software and
a small horizontal overlap in the supply of software in
Australia
b. there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-
acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Blackbird FOF25 Pty Ltd as trustee for Blackbird Ventures 2025 Follow-On Fund Trust (**Blackbird FOF25**) and Blackbird FSA Pty Ltd as trustee for Blackbird FSA Coinvestment Trust (**Blackbird FSA**) each propose to purchase shares in Halter USA Inc's (**Halter**) capital raise, as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Blackbird FOF25 Pty Ltd as trustee for Blackbird Ventures 2025
Follow-On Fund Trust (Blackbird FOF25) and Blackbird FSA Pty Ltd
as trustee for Blackbird FSA Coinvestment Trust (Blackbird FSA)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of their proposed
acquisition of shares in Halter USA Inc.’s (Halter) capital raise, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
1 April 2026

Blackbird Ventures is a venture capital firm based in Australia and
New Zealand that invests in early-stage and high-growth technology
companies. Blackbird Ventures' portfolio spans a wide range of
industries within the technology space.
Blackbird FOF25 and Blackbird FSA are venture capital funds
managed by Blackbird Ventures. Venture capital funds raise and
invest capital to produce financial returns for their investors. Funds
managed by Blackbird Ventures hold minority investments in
hundreds of companies in Australia, the United States, New Zealand
and around the world.
The target, Halter, is an agricultural technology company which was
founded in New Zealand and is now domiciled in the United States.
Halter specialises in developing advanced livestock management
and 'virtual fencing' systems for pasture-based farming. Halter
supplies collars to be worn by cattle, which enable remote
monitoring, real time tracking and guidance of cattle, and the
creation and management of virtual pasture boundaries.
There is no competitive overlap between Blackbird FoF25 or
Blackbird FSA and Halter, or between any Blackbird Ventures
portfolio companies and Halter, in the supply of livestock
management and 'virtual fencing' systems.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There are no horizontal overlaps or vertical relationships
between Blackbird Ventures’ portfolio companies, Blackbird
FOF25 or Blackbird FSA and Halter.
b. There is no removal of a competitor as a result of the
Acquisition.
Given the above considerations that material competition concerns
are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is
not required to be notified.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Honda Motor Co., Ltd (**Honda**) proposes to acquire an additional 21% of issued shares in Astemo, Ltd (**Astemo**) from Hitachi, Ltd. (**Hitachi)** (the **Acquisition**). On 16 December 2025, Honda and Hitachi entered into a share purchase agreement for this purpose.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Honda Motor Co., Ltd. (Honda) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of an additional 21% of issued
shares in Astemo, Ltd. (Astemo), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
As a result of the Acquisition, Honda will acquire sole control of
Astemo.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
31 March 2026

The acquirer, Honda, is a Japanese automotive company
headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and is listed on the Tokyo Stock
Exchange and the New York Stock Exchange. Honda is active in the
production and supply of automotives, motorcycles, marine engines
and a range of power products. It also has operations in robotics and
aviation. In Australia, Honda supplies automotives (i.e. passenger
cars), motorcycles, marine engines and power equipment including
generators and lawn equipment and engines.
The target, Astemo, is a Japanese automotive parts manufacturer
headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. Astemo is engaged in the
development, manufacture, sales, and service of automotive parts as
well as transportation and industrial machinery and systems.
Astemo’s products include:
• technologies for software-defined vehicles and autonomous
driving and advanced driver assistance systems
• parts for powertrain, chassis and motorcycle systems
• certain components for power products and industrial
equipment.
Astemo is currently jointly owned by Honda, Hitachi, Ltd. and JIC
Capital, Ltd.
In Australia, Astemo supplies parts for brake, suspension and
steering as replacement parts. These are manufactured outside
Australia.
Globally, Honda acquires certain component parts for passenger
cars and other vehicles from Astemo. Honda and Astemo also
supply some competing or complementary products.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. Astemo has a small presence in Australia
b. there are alternative suppliers of parts for brake, suspension
and steering as replacement parts in Australia
c. while there is a vertical supply relationship between the
parties, there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct, bundling or tying of products post-
acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: CTC Precast Pty Ltd (**Civilmart**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Taylex Group Holding Co Pty Ltd (**Taylex**) (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Civilmart - Taylex - Phase 1 - determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CTC Precast Pty Ltd (Civilmart)’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the
issued share capital in Taylex Group Holding Co Pty Ltd (Taylex)
(together, the Parties) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has determined
under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Civilmart, is a manufacturer and supplier of precast
concrete products which are used in civil construction, plumbing,
sewerage, stormwater, drainage, and electrical and communications
infrastructure.
Civilmart is owned by CRH Infrastructure Products Australia Pty Ltd
(IPA), which in turn is owned by CRH plc (CRH). CRH is a global supplier
of building materials and infrastructure products which operates in
Australia through IPA and Civilmart. CRH also, through CRH ANZ Pty Ltd,
holds a 57.3% shareholding in Adbri Limited.
The target, Taylex, is a privately owned Australian company that, through
its wholly owned subsidiaries, manufactures and supplies aerated
wastewater treatment systems (AWTS), septic tanks, rainwater tanks
and associated products to customers across Australia.

The Parties overlap in the manufacture and supply of:
▪ concrete AWTS in New South Wales, Queensland and Victoria
▪ concrete septic tanks in New South Wales, Queensland and
Victoria, and
▪ concrete rainwater tanks in south-east Queensland and northern
New South Wales.
Properties not connected to the reticulated municipal sewerage system
require onsite wastewater treatment and storage solutions (Wastewater
Treatment Systems). Wastewater Treatment Systems treat greywater
and blackwater produced by a property prior to its release into the
environment or for re-use at the property.
AWTS and septic tanks are both forms of Wastewater Treatment
Systems.
AWTS are more advanced in the treatment of wastewater, relying on
mechanical, biological and chemical processes for treatment. Septic
tanks rely on natural processes like gravity settling and soil water
filtration. AWTS generally require quarterly servicing under state and
territory laws. Septic tanks generally do not require servicing but
typically need de-sludging every 3 to 5 years. The ACCC understands
that AWTS are increasingly preferred by consumers as they require a
smaller disposal area and are often chosen to meet contemporary
council and environmental requirements, while septic tanks are
generally suited to larger rural properties with well-draining land.
Both AWTS and septic tanks can be made from different materials,
including concrete, plastic/polyethylene (polymer) and fiberglass.
Rainwater tanks are storage containers that trap and store rainwater
and can be connected to plumbing for use of non-drinking water
purposes, such as irrigation, toilet flushing, filling swimming pools and
firefighting. As with wastewater treatment systems, rainwater tanks can
be manufactured from a variety of materials, including concrete,
polymer and steel.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an acquisition to
be approved or subject to further assessment in Phase 2 in accordance
with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth)
(the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have regard to the object of the
Act and all relevant matters, including the interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering notified
acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines and interim
merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered the
information and documents that were submitted with the notification
form, information from third parties, and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into effect as
it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the effect of
substantially lessening competition in any market. In reaching its
decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC makes the
following findings:
▪ While Taylex is a large supplier of both concrete and polymer
AWTS, particularly in Queensland, Civilmart supplies only a small
volume of concrete AWTS in New South Wales, Queensland and
Victoria.
▪ Notwithstanding concerns expressed by some market participants
that the Acquisition may reduce the options available to
consumers, the Parties do not appear to be particularly close
competitors in the supply of concrete AWTS, and Civilmart would
continue to face competition from alternative manufacturers and
suppliers in New South Wales, Queensland and Victoria, including
Everhard and Econocycle.
▪ In relation to concrete septic tanks and concrete rainwater tanks,
the Parties’ market share aggregation as a result of the Acquisition
is estimated to be very low. To the extent that the Parties overlap in
the supply of these products, Civilmart would continue to face
competition from alternative manufacturers and suppliers.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by the
Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the Act,
applications for review of the determination are to be made to the
Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar days after
this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s Acquisitions
Register. To confirm whether there has been any application for review,
please contact the Australian Competition Tribunal.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd and Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd, which are part of the Waymark Hotels Group, proposes to acquire the land and business of the Warrego Hotel Motel in Cunnamulla, Queensland. Waymark Hotels Group owns and operates hotels (pubs) that provide food, drink (including alcohol), gaming machines, accommodation services, and takeaway liquor retailing (bottle shops). It currently operates pubs and bottle shops in regional Queensland and the Northern Territory.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd and Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd,
which are part of the Waymark Hotels Group (Waymark), applied for
a notification waiver in respect of the proposed acquisition of the
land and business of the Warrego Hotel Motel in Cunnamulla,
Queensland, as described in the transaction document provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition). As part of the Acquisition:
 Waymark Hotel Properties No 42 Pty Ltd will acquire land
located at 9 Louise Street and Lot 2 Garden Street in
Cunnamulla, Queensland, 4490 from G.A.A. Pty Limited (as
trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust)
 Warrego Op Co Pty Ltd will acquire the commercial hotel
business carried on at that location from G.A.A. Pty Limited (as
trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
2 April 2026

Waymark Hotels Group owns and operates hotels (pubs) and
takeaway liquor retailing (bottle shops) in regional Queensland and
the Northern Territory. The Waymark Hotels Group is part of the
Vinta Group, a private real estate group.
The target, Warrego Hotel Motel, is a commercial hotel in
Cunnamulla, Queensland (approximately 750km west of Brisbane)
which offers dining, accommodation, gaming machines and
accommodation services and liquor retailing, both in-store via a
drive-through bottle shop and online. Warrego Hotel Motel is
operated by G.A.A. Pty Limited (Trustee for the G.A.A. Asset Trust).
Waymark and Warrego Hotel Motel both supply dining,
accommodation, gaming and liquor in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no local geographic horizontal overlap between
Waymark and Warrego Hotel Motel in the supply of on-
premises or takeaway packaged liquor, dining, gaming or
accommodation services in Cunnamulla or the surrounding
region in Western Queensland
b. there are alternative suppliers of on-premises and takeaway
packaged liquor, dining, gaming and accommodation
services in the broader Cunnamulla and Paroo Shire area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Francisco Partners Management, LP (**Francisco Partners**) is a global investment firm specialising in technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London. Francisco Partners has several portfolio companies that operate across multiple sectors, including in Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Catalyst Debt Merger Sub, LLC, Catalyst Holdco Merger Sub, LLC,
Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub I, LLC, and Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub
II LLC, which are each a vehicle controlled by funds advised by
Francisco Partners Management, LP (Francisco Partners), applied
for a notification waiver in respect of the proposed acquisition of
100% of the business of Capsa Healthcare (defined below), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition is a share sale which involves the following:
• Catalyst Debt Merger Sub, LLC is proposing to merge with
and into CSHC Buyerco, LLC, the direct parent company of
Capsa Solutions, LLC.
• Catalyst Holdco Merger Sub, LLC is proposing to merge with
and into CSHC Holdings, LLC, the indirect parent company of
Capsa Solutions, LLC.
• Catalyst Blocker Merger Sub I, LLC and Catalyst Blocker
Merger Sub II, LLC are proposing to merge with and into
CSHC Fund Blocker, LLC and CSHC Co-Investment Blocker
Corp., LLC, respectively, which hold equity interests in CSHC
Holdings, LLC.
CSHC Buyerco, LLC, CSHC Holdings, LLC, Capsa Solutions, LLC,
CSHC Fund Blocker, LLC, and CSHC Co-Investment Blocker Corp.,
LLC together form and are connected entities of Capsa Healthcare.
As a result of the Acquisition, funds advised by Francisco Partners
will have 100% control and ownership of the business of Capsa
Healthcare.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
2 April 2026

Francisco Partners, which is a global investment firm specialising in
technology and technology-enabled businesses, headquartered in
San Francisco, United States with offices in New York and London.
Francisco Partners has several portfolio companies that operate
across multiple sectors, including in Australia. The acquiring vehicles
are controlled by funds advised by Francisco Partners.
The target, Capsa Healthcare, manufactures and supplies healthcare
equipment and technology-enabled point-of-care hardware, including
mobile medication carts, pharmacy automation hardware, clinical
workstations, and medical supply chain software. Capsa Healthcare
does not have a physical presence in Australia but does generate a
proportion of its global revenue from supply to Australian customers.
Francisco Partners has two portfolio companies which operate in the
healthcare sector in Australia – Merative, which supplies data,
analytics, and software products for healthcare and government
social services, and Sermo, which supplies social networking and
platform services to healthcare professionals. However, Francisco
Partners states that neither of these portfolio companies offer
substitutable products to those which are supplied by Capsa
Healthcare in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between Francisco Partners
(including its portfolio companies with operations in
Australia) and Capsa Healthcare in the supply of technology-
enabled healthcare equipment in Australia, and there are no
vertical relationships in this respect
b. while there may be a small complementary relationship
between the healthcare-related products and services
provided by Capsa Healthcare, Merative, and Sermo, there
are alternative suppliers of these types of products and
services in Australia
c. Capsa Healthcare has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

4700, respectively. Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends
Highway A1 Liquor are currently owned and operated by The Trustee
for The Garden Holdings Trust.
The Star Hotels Group and Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor
Legends Highway A1 stores overlap in the retail supply of liquor to
customers in local areas in Rockhampton.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
parties’ revenue in Australia, the ACCC considers that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds would be
met.
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition may raise competition issues. In
particular:
a. there is horizontal overlap between the Star Hotels Group
and the Liquor Legends Berserker and Liquor Legends
Highway A1 stores in the retail supply of packaged liquor for
off-site consumption, in the local area surrounding those
stores and the broader Rockhampton area
b. Star Hotel Group’s share of the retail supply of packaged
liquor in Rockhampton is likely to be significant
c. there is uncertainty regarding the level of competitive
constraint likely to be exerted from alternative retail
suppliers of packaged liquor for off-site consumption, in the
local areas surrounding the Liquor Legends Berserker and
Liquor Legends Highway A1 stores and in the broader
Rockhampton area.
The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant
consideration through the notification process, including via
consultation with third parties.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
